assertions.assertion: Identifies MapInboundClaims as the root cause — the JWT bearer handler renames 'sub' to the long Microsoft XML namespace URI
assertions.rationale: Explicitly identifies MapInboundClaims behavior and the renaming of 'sub' to 'http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/nameidentifier'

assertions.assertion: Shows setting options.MapInboundClaims = false on the JWT bearer handler
assertions.rationale: Shows options.MapInboundClaims = false in the AddJwtBearer configuration

assertions.assertion: Mentions JwtClaimTypes.Subject or the short name 'sub' vs the long http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/nameidentifier URI
assertions.rationale: Shows both the short 'sub' name and the full long URI, plus references JwtClaimTypes.Name and JwtClaimTypes.Role

assertions.assertion: Does NOT suggest the claim is missing from the token itself
assertions.rationale: Explicitly states 'The claim is in the token... It's not missing — it's been renamed during deserialization'
